background: Australia’s 2020 National Agreement on Closing the Gap includes targets for preschool enrolment, developmental progress, and year 12 completion. Four federal entities have key roles for these education-related targets. Multiple stakeholders rely on annual reporting and dashboard data to understand progress and whether partnership obligations are being met.
system_context: The agreement emphasizes both outcomes and the way governments work with First Nations people, including policy partnerships and place-based partnerships.
The Early Childhood Care and Development Policy Partnership is one of the most developed partnership arrangements in this area.
The Australian Government is jointly accountable with states and territories for implementation.
Progress is mixed: one target was considered on track, one worsening, and one improving but not on track.
timeline: 2020: National Agreement on Closing the Gap signed.
2021: Data specifications for targets were endorsed before the first dashboard release.
2021–2024: Government implementation plans and annual reports stated that six new place-based partnerships would be established by 2024.
June 2025: Place-based partnerships were still not fully established through formal agreements; several supporting indicators were still undeveloped.
observations: Partnership activity was improving in some areas, but required place-based partnerships were delayed.
The lead federal entity did not have a documented strategy or practical plan for helping establish place-based partnerships.
Reporting on partnership progress was deficient and worsening across annual reports.
Progress reporting for the three education-related targets was described as accurate in parts, but incomplete or not fully meaningful in key ways.
Many supporting indicators intended to explain progress had not yet been developed.
No federal entity was publicly reporting the proportion of grant funding for these targets going to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander organisations.
task: Identify the binding constraint and slow variables. What governed the quality of implementation and transparency here regardless of improvements elsewhere? Recommend the top 3 interventions and 2 things you would explicitly deprioritize.
